rationale: This draft tests whether content-ranking correction and platform-level governance can be represented as two distinct recurrent cycles without treating engagement or synchronization as alignment.

scenario.title: Social Platform Under Engagement Pressure
scenario.summary: A social platform balancing engagement optimization, content correction, governance adaptation, user feedback, and cumulative trust debt.
scenario.viableRegion: Engagement that preserves user agency, information quality, safety, and institutional trust
scenario.hiddenConstraint: Ranking metrics incompletely represent long-horizon user and societal effects
scenario.debtMechanism: Unresolved moderation failures, incentive distortions, appeals backlog, and trust erosion
scenario.irreversibleMechanism: Severe harm, entrenched manipulation, institutional capture, or lasting community loss
warningConditions: Engagement pressure rises while trustworthy feedback weakens
Moderation and appeals debt accumulates across ranking cycles
Radial excursion grows despite apparently stable engagement
scenario.ruptureCondition: Cumulative trust and safety debt pushes modeled excursion beyond the critical viability boundary.
scenario.recoveryCondition: Engagement pressure falls while feedback, correction, and governance capacity remain strong enough to contract excursion and repay debt.
scenario.plainLanguageInterpretation: A platform can improve a short-term engagement metric while making future correction more difficult and narrowing the set of trustworthy operating states.

minor.label: Rank → observe → correct
minor.description: Fast content-ranking, feedback, moderation, and correction cycle

major.label: Govern → adapt
major.description: Slower institutional adaptation to users, adversaries, policy, and social context

presets.name: Deliberative balance
presets.description: Feedback and correction remain ahead of engagement divergence.

presets.name: Engagement at risk
presets.description: High engagement pressure and weak feedback narrow the correction margin.

presets.name: Trust recovery
presets.description: Lower pressure and stronger governance support debt repayment.

evidence.hypothesis: The platform remains viable when ranking-level correction and governance-level adaptation both recur and correction capacity remains ahead of engagement-driven divergence.
assumptions: The ranking and governance cycles are meaningfully distinct and recurrent.
The canonical variables are qualitative mappings rather than calibrated platform measurements.
Synchronization or engagement alone is not treated as alignment.
The selected thresholds are illustrative and require domain review before publication.
references: Sori, A. (2026). Toroidal Geometry in ATS/AANA/AIx, revised phase-coordinate edition.
Viability Torus Lab architecture and status-threshold documentation.

interventions.label: Improve feedback and governance while reducing engagement pressure
